title: cycle-965 FAIL: builder attempted a bare commit (ship-gate correctly denied on any branch) → no deliverable commit → build rejected; builder must use the sanctioned cycle-commit path
why: Cycle-965 (pipeline-hardening batch) FAILed at the build->audit gate: build-report 'Worktree commit was ship-gate-denied (bare git commit correctly refused on any branch)'. The builder agent tried to commit its own work with a bare commit; the ship-gate hook (fires on ALL commits repo-wide, incl worktrees) correctly denied it, so the cycle produced no valid deliverable commit. Cycles 968/969 committed fine via the sanctioned path — so this is builder-behavior divergence, not the fix code being wrong (a FALSE-code-RED). The delivered work may be sound but was thrown away for using the wrong commit mechanism.
how_to_apply: Root-cause: the builder should NOT self-commit; the orchestrator/ship phase owns the cycle commit. Either (a) strengthen the builder persona/prompt contract to never run git commit (leave commits to ship), and/or (b) make the ship-gate denial RECOVERABLE for the build phase — on a denied bare commit in a worktree, the orchestrator absorbs the uncommitted work (like leak recovery stages it) rather than failing the cycle. Prefer (b): the builder's SOURCE is intact in the worktree; a wrong commit mechanism should not discard sound work. Add a regression test (builder emits bare commit → work preserved + cycle proceeds).
evidence: .evolve/runs/cycle-965/build-report.md 'Worktree commit was ship-gate-denied (bare ... correctly refused on any branch)'; cycle-state completed=[scout,triage,fault-localization,tdd,retro] (no build/audit).
